title: Avoid Using "DateUtils.truncate" from Apache Commons Lang Library
description: The "DateUtils.truncate" method from Apache Commons Lang library is not recommended due to inefficiency. Instead, consider using the "truncatedTo" method from the "ZonedDateTime" class, which provides a faster way to truncate dates. This rule is specifically relevant to Java 8 and later versions, where "ZonedDateTime" is available.
recommendation: Replace the usage of "DateUtils.truncate" with "ZonedDateTime.truncatedTo" method for date truncation in Java 8 or later versions.
impact: Using the "DateUtils.truncate" method can lead to slower performance due to inefficient date truncation. By switching to the "ZonedDateTime.truncatedTo" method, you can improve the speed of date truncation operations in your code.

checks.message: Rule: Avoid Using "DateUtils.truncate" from Apache Commons Lang Library
